text: the following video is of laparoscopic selenectomy from government medical college srinagar post graduate department of surgery and critical care medicine in the short video that you are about to see We are demonstrating laparoscopic splenectomy.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to dissect the omentum in front of the spleen, revealing two distinct tissue layers.
The surgeon continues to use the harmonic scalpel to dissect tissue in front of the spleen, utilizing its hemostatic and dissecting capabilities to separate the identified layers.
The surgeon uses the Enseal instrument near the stomach to manage the short gastric vessels, demonstrating its hemostatic capabilities.
The splenic artery is visually identified at the six o'clock position relative to the surgical field.
The surgeon uses the Enseal device to dissect the short gastric vessels away from the stomach, ensuring hemostasis during the laparoscopic splenectomy procedure.
The surgeon turns attention to the hilum of the spleen, using endoshears to dissect the vasculature within this area, carefully separating the vessels from surrounding tissue.
A vessel near the spleen is identified and skeletonized, with hemolog clips applied proximally to achieve ligation.
The splenic artery is visible with an early branching pattern, presenting a more complex anatomical configuration than typical, which may complicate the dissection process.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to dissect between the vessels, clearly identifying the splenic vein and branches of the splenic artery.
The surgeon carefully dissects behind the splenic artery and its branches, lifting them off surrounding tissue and applying hemolog clips for ligation.
The surgeon uses a right angle dissector to carefully dissect and free the main branch of the splenic artery, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon uses harmonic shears to cut the vessel off the hilum after it has been clipped.
The surgeon applies pressure to the superior aspect of the spleen, visibly redirecting some blood back into the circulation.
The surgeon addresses the splenic vein to prevent congestion, ensuring proper management of the vasculature during the splenectomy procedure.
The surgeon performs further dissection of the splenic hilum towards the superior pole of the spleen using endoshears, carefully separating the tissue in this area.
The surgeon carefully separates the spleen from the hilum, ensuring that surrounding structures are not injured during the process.
The surgeon dissects the adhesions on the posterior aspect of the spleen, allowing the spleen to be lifted off the hilum.
The surgeon addresses the adhesions near the superior pole of the spleen, freeing the spleen from these attachments to facilitate further manipulation.
Thank you.
